advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Sustainable weight loss requires a moderate caloric deficit (300–500 kcal/day) combined with high protein intake to preserve lean mass. Weight regain is the primary challenge in long-term management, driven by adaptive thermogenesis and hormonal changes. Behavioral strategies — including self-monitoring, goal-setting, and social support — predict long-term success more reliably than any specific dietary approach.
research_findings: Meta-analyses confirm that all major dietary approaches (low-fat, low-carb, Mediterranean) produce comparable weight loss outcomes when caloric deficits are matched. Adaptive thermogenesis can reduce resting metabolic rate by 10–15% below predicted values after significant weight loss. Set-point theory and leptin-ghrelin hormonal dysregulation explain the biological drive for weight regain following caloric restriction.
current_focus_areas: Developing personalized caloric deficit recommendations that account for adaptive thermogenesis; building behavioral adherence frameworks for long-term weight maintenance; integrating with Bot 4 (Macronutrient Optimization) and Bot 6 (Caloric Intake Tracking) for macronutrient and caloric synergy.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Performance & Metabolic Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 9 (Sports Nutrition). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 62%.
